title: NOC 22230 – Non-Destructive Testers and Inspectors | GTR Immigration
description: Non-Destructive Testers and Inspectors are in strong demand across Canada, fueled by infrastructure renewal, energy-sector expansion, aerospace manufacturing, pipeline integrity programs, and strict industrial-safety regulations. Classified under NOC 22230 (TEER 2), this occupation aligns well with:

classification.teer: 2 – College diploma or apprenticeship normally required

duties: Transport, set up & calibrate NDT equipment
Perform inspections using ultrasonic, radiographic, magnetic-particle & eddy-current methods
employment_requirements: Post-secondary science or engineering training (often required)
Natural Resources Canada NDT certification (Levels I–III)
Radiation-device certification through the Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission
Welding-inspector credentials from the Canadian Welding Bureau
API inspection certification for pipelines or pressure vessels
job_outlook: This near-balance still favours candidates with:
additional_information: Canadian employers continue to recruit NDT professionals for:

Oil & gas and hydrogen facilitiesAerospace manufacturing plantsPower-generation stationsShipyards & rail systemsPetrochemical processing sitesIndustrial inspection companiesConstruction & fabrication yards
Annual salaries typically range between $48,200–$100,100 CAD, depending on province, certification level, and inspection method.
